restaurant_group: Yong Fu Group (multiple Shanghai locations + Beijing, HK)
signature_dishes: 雪菜大黄鱼 (yellow croaker with pickled mustard greens)
红膏炝蟹 (raw drunken red-roe crab)
vibe_and_space: Quiet, classy Chinese fine-dining — private rooms dominant, polished service, no music. Built for slow seafood-heavy dinners.
one_paragraph_recommendation: Shanghai's reference-standard Ningbo seafood restaurant — Michelin one-star since 2017, kept through 2026. The 红膏炝蟹 (raw drunken crab with red roe) is the signature pilgrimage dish; the 雪菜黄鱼 is the everyday classic. Recommend for any business dinner where Cantonese feels too obvious and 本帮 too heavy — the Ningbo profile (saltier, seafood-forward, less sweet than Shanghainese) reads as sophisticated to local guests. The Huangpu sibling is more convenient if 虹口 is far; the 浦东 'Yongfu Mini' (甬府小鲜) is the Bib Gourmand casual format.
